summary: Tested major multi-user auth refactor (Emergent Google OAuth). Backend: 21/21 pytest passed (auth-required endpoints, isolation, logout, session exchange). Frontend: login redirect, cookie-based auth, ProfileGate routing, sidebar user-card, logout, and User-A/User-B isolation all verified via Playwright. Found and fixed one critical 500 bug: User.created_at validation when seeded via mongosh new Date() (BSON datetime).
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py User.created_at was Optional[str] only — broke /api/auth/me with 500 when users were mongosh-seeded with `new Date()` per /app/auth_testing.md (BSON Date -> Python datetime fails Pydantic str validation). Fixed by coercing datetime->isoformat in make_require_user before constructing User. Real OAuth flow already stored isoformat strings so production users were unaffected, but the documented testing playbook would 500 every time.
server.py is 668 lines and could be split into routers (auth done; quests/profile/stats/awakening could move to separate modules) for maintainability.

compute_stats() is called twice inside _complete_quest_internal (before & after); plus once again from check_achievements. Three full re-aggregations per quest completion. Acceptable now; flag for optimisation as data grows.

session_exchange does not validate that response from EMERGENT_OAUTH_SESSION_URL contains required keys (email, session_token) — a malformed upstream response would 500 instead of 401. Low risk but worth a guard.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all flows: login redirect, cookie auth, ProfileGate, dashboard sidebar, navigation, logout, isolation)

test_credentials: Mongosh-seeded test users in /app/memory/test_credentials.md. USER_A token=test_session_A_1778158753850 (has profile seeded), USER_B token=test_session_B_1778158753850 (no profile, hits ProfileGate -> /awakening). Frontend cookies: domain=xp-goal.preview.emergentagent.com path=/ httpOnly secure sameSite=None.
seed_data_creation: Two users + sessions seeded directly via mongosh. User A also got a profile doc seeded so dashboard renders. Pytest fixtures create their own TEST_-prefixed users and clean up after the run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-user auth refactor verified end-to-end. Backend test file: /app/backend/tests/test_multiuser_auth.py covers (a) all protected endpoints return 401 unauthed, (b) Bearer-token /me works, (c) two-user isolation via direct mongo seeding, (d) cross-user complete returns 404, (e) /api/reset is user-scoped, (f) logout invalidates session, (g) /auth/session with bogus session_id -> 401. Real Google OAuth flow can NOT be exercised in automation; cookie injection from mongosh-seeded sessions is the only path. Fix applied: auth.py:_dep now coerces datetime created_at to isoformat string before constructing User pydantic model. Re-run command: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=$(grep REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL frontend/.env | cut -d= -f2) python -m pytest backend/tests/test_multiuser_auth.py -v
rca of the issue: Symptom: /api/auth/me returned 500 for any mongosh-seeded test user, blocking entire frontend auth testing. Root cause: backend/auth.py User pydantic model declared created_at: Optional[str], but the auth_testing.md playbook seeds users with MongoDB `new Date()` which Motor returns to Python as a datetime instance — Pydantic v2 strict-mode rejects datetime as str. Real-flow OAuth path was unaffected because session_exchange._upsert_user calls .isoformat() before insert. Fix: in make_require_user, convert created_at to isoformat string if it is a datetime, before User(**user_doc). Verified: /me returns 200 with cookie + ProfileGate + Dashboard render correctly.
